steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The goal is to emphasize the historical significance of Stephen Amos’s enlistment date. The decisive clue in the notes is that only a small number of Filipino-descent sailors served in the Civil War at all — placing Amos within that rare group is what makes his date significant.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Both Stephen Amos and Raphael Ignases were US Navy sailors of Filipino descent, but Amos enlisted in the Navy in 1863, two years later than Ignases.: This compares Amos’s date to Ignases’s date but never explains why either date matters historically — later does not mean significant. ✗
  • Choice B — Stephen Amos was a US Navy sailor of Filipino descent, along with Raphael Ignases, who was born in the Philippines around 1834.: This mentions Ignases’s birth year instead of Amos’s enlistment date and provides no connection to the Civil War’s historical context. ✗
  • Choice C — Stephen Amos enlisted in the US Navy in 1863, making him one of the few sailors of Filipino descent to serve in the US Civil War (1861–1865).: This gives the enlistment date and directly connects it to the rarity established by the notes — exactly the historical significance the question asks for. ✓
  • Choice D — When Stephen Amos enlisted in the US Navy in November 1863, he joined sailors such as Raphael Ignases, who had been born in the Philippines around 1834.: This provides Amos’s enlistment date but frames it only as joining another sailor, not as belonging to a historically rare group. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
